etymology.description: 子(子) (zǐ, "son") + 系(系) (xì, "continue") = offspring that continues the family line -> grandson
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 子(子) (zǐ, "son") and 系(系) (xì, "continue") to represent the idea of a descendant who continues the family line, specifically a grandson.
etymology.simplification: Simplified to 孙(孙) by changing 系(系) to 小(小) to reduce complexity.

examples.chinese: 他的孙子(他的孫子)每天放学后都会来找他(每天放學後都會來找他)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de sūnzi měitiān fàngxué hòu dōu huì lái zhǎo tā.
examples.english: His grandson comes to find him every day after school.

examples.chinese: 孙悟空(孫悟空)是《西游记》中的一个角色(是《西游記》中的一個角色)。
examples.pinyin: Sūn Wùkōng shì 《Xīyóujì》 zhōng de yī gè juésè.
examples.english: Sun Wukong is a character in 'Journey to the West'.

examples.chinese: 他已经有三个孙子了(他已經有三個孫子了)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǐjīng yǒu sān gè sūnzi le.
